title: The Economy, Plain and Simple
summary: This is a Bank of Canada landing page that aggregates educational articles on economics, not a specific research report or data release. It links to short explainers on topics including payment evolution, economic uncertainty, inflation, productivity, labour participation, and interest rate transmission. The page contains no original macro analysis, policy decisions, or market-moving data.
key_points: This is a navigation/listing page, not an archiveable research document
The page links to educational content rather than containing substantive macro analysis
Listed articles cover general economic topics: inflation, interest rates, productivity, labour markets, grocery prices, gasoline prices
The most recent article linked is from September 12, 2025 ('The evolution of how we pay for things')
No specific macro catalysts, policy decisions, or market-moving information is present
Content is designed for public economic education, not professional research
why_it_matters: This page has no direct relevance for finance research or RAG applications. It is a navigation hub for consumer-facing educational content from the Bank of Canada. It does not contain policy statements, data releases, or analytical commentary that would support macro catalyst research.
notes: NON-ARCHIVEABLE: This source is a listing page for the Bank of Canada's educational content series. It does not contain specific macro catalyst information, policy decisions, or market-moving data. The linked articles touch on macro topics (inflation, interest rates) but the page itself is merely a collection index. Recommend reviewing individual linked articles separately if substantive content is needed. The source_type (listing_page) indicates this should not be treated as an archiveable research document per CORE RULE 6.
